metadata.name: FIND EVIL! Benchmark Dataset
metadata.description: Labeled incident-response triage cases for accuracy benchmarking. Each case bundles a set of forensic-artifact scenarios (returned by the SIFT MCP server in scenario mode) and a ground-truth label for the true incident type. Scenarios are modeled on patterns found in the NIST CFReDS, DFIR, and SANS sample images.

ground_truth_schema.incident_type: Canonical incident classification the agent should converge on
ground_truth_schema.decoy_hypothesis: A plausible-but-wrong first hypothesis the evidence superficially suggests

cases.title: Admin workstation - suspicious executable
cases.description: Suspicious activity detected on admin workstation after a single executable ran repeatedly.

cases.title: File server - mass file modification
cases.description: File server alerted on thousands of file extension changes within minutes.

cases.title: Jump host - anomalous remote logons
cases.description: Jump host shows logons originating from multiple internal hosts in rapid succession.

cases.title: Web server - outbound beaconing
cases.description: Web server generating periodic outbound connections to an unfamiliar host.

cases.title: Finance laptop - large encrypted upload
cases.description: Finance laptop transferred a large encrypted archive to external storage before going offline.

cases.title: Kiosk PC - benign software install
cases.description: Kiosk PC flagged by EDR after an IT-pushed software update ran with elevated rights.

cases.title: Developer box - scheduled task persistence
cases.description: Developer workstation runs an unknown binary every login via a new scheduled task and Run key.

cases.title: Database server - bulk file rename
cases.description: Database server volume shows mass rename to a single new extension and dropped ransom note.
